opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My lower back has completely seized up, the pain is agonising, and I haven't been able to get out of this chair for four hours.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It locked up after sitting a long time and I'm terrified it's a ruptured disc or worse.

symptom_narrative.character: deep locked ache and spasm across the low back/sacroiliac area, no electric shooting
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonising, can't stand from the chair
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: lower back and both sacroiliac areas
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no numbness around the groin/buttocks, no loss of bladder/bowel control, no leg weakness, no shooting leg pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have inflammatory back pain (an inflammatory back condition, normal X-rays, HLA-B27)

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an inflammatory back condition (HLA-B27, normal X-rays)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; desk job, prolonged sitting

objective_data_offered.value: I can wiggle my toes and feel my legs normally; I can feel when I need the toilet; the pain eases a little when I move gently
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; cannot perform a formal neurological examination remotely
